spec_match.rationale: Uses `position-anchor` and `anchor()` for the CSS-anchored path and offers a JS getBoundingClientRect fallback for comparison. But the `anchor-name: var(--anchor-name)` pattern uses a CSS custom property at anchor declaration — this is unusual and may not match Chrome 135's accepted syntax (anchor names are typically literal `--name` declarations, not pulled through `var()`).

interactive.rationale: Scenario tabs, scroll-stage with anchored terms, and a JS-fallback comparison mode. Hover and scroll drive the interaction.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb promises an API-docs page with anchor tooltips and JS fallback comparison — delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Reviewer note: file was only partially read; could not assess all scenarios. Need to verify whether remembered-offset is specifically exercised vs just plain anchor positioning.

references.rationale: Need to verify see-also section content — file was only partially read.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, semantic probe pill states, color-mix for tab and probe states.

openQuestions.title: Verify `anchor-name: var(--anchor-name)` is valid
openQuestions.detail: The CSS uses `anchor-name: var(--anchor-name)` to vary the name per element via a custom property. The spec is explicit that anchor-name is a list of custom-ident values — whether the browser accepts a `var()` substitution into a custom-ident position needs verification. If it doesn't, the anchor names never resolve and the position-anchor links all fail silently.

openQuestions.title: Specifically exercise the v135 remembered-offset feature
openQuestions.detail: The page demonstrates anchor positioning generally and a JS fallback. To match the feature slug, add a clear demonstration of `position-try-options: most-scroll-position` vs without — that's the specific spec change for v135, not anchor positioning in general (which shipped in 125).

openQuestions.title: Read the rest of the page to complete the critique
openQuestions.detail: This critique was generated from the first ~100 lines only. A follow-up pass should read the scenarios, fallback logic, and verify see-also section content.

openQuestions.title: Add quantitative comparison vs JS fallback
openQuestions.detail: Lede promises a comparison with JS getBoundingClientRect fallback. Add a frame-time or jank counter so the perf delta (CSS-driven vs JS-rAF-driven) is visible, not just qualitatively described.

summary: Most ambitious concept of the four — API-docs page with CSS-anchored tooltips and a JS fallback comparison. Two concerns: the `anchor-name: var(--anchor-name)` pattern needs spec verification, and the page doesn't clearly isolate the v135 remembered-offset feature vs general anchor positioning that shipped earlier.
